================================================================================
CONCLUSION
================================================================================
Online visibility (top 10% most shared) significantly predicts higher citation impact across areas.
Gender composition interacts differently: 
 - Computer Science: teams with female first and/or last authors receive more citations,
   but female-last-author teams benefit less from visibility than MM teams (negative interaction).
 - Engineering: only female-female teams have a citation advantage; interaction is positive but not robust.
 - Social Sciences: gender-diverse teams (FM, MF) have more citations; visibility benefits all equally.
Thus, while online visibility can help reduce the citation gap, its effect depends on field-specific dynamics and team gender composition.
